info.title: Sorcha.Cli
info.description: Administrative CLI tool for managing Sorcha distributed ledger platform

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Sorcha CLI - Administrative tool for managing Sorcha distributed ledger platform

options.name: --profile
options.description: Configuration profile to use (uses active profile from config if not specified)

options.name: --output
options.description: Output format (table, json, csv, yaml) [default: table]

options.name: --machine-readable
options.description: Wrap output in standard JSON envelope for automation [default: False]

options.name: --create-sp
options.description: Create service principal for automation [default: False]

options.name: --non-interactive
options.description: Non-interactive mode (all options must be provided) [default: False]

commands.name: org
commands.description:
Manage organizations

Examples:
  sorcha org list
  sorcha org get --id <org-id>
  sorcha org create --name "My Org" --subdomain myorg

options.name: --subdomain
options.description: Unique subdomain (3-50 alphanumeric characters with hyphens)

commands.name: user
commands.description:
Manage users within organizations

Examples:
  sorcha user list
  sorcha user create --email user@example.com --first-name John --last-name Doe
  sorcha user get --id <user-id>

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all users in an organization

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a new user in an organization

options.name: --roles
options.description: Comma-separated list of roles (replaces existing roles)

commands.name: principal
commands.description:
Manage service principals within organizations

Examples:
  sorcha principal list
  sorcha principal create --name "API Client" --org-id <id>

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all service principals in an organization

commands.name: get
commands.description: Get a service principal by client ID

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a new service principal in an organization

commands.name: rotate-secret
commands.description: Rotate the client secret for a service principal

commands.name: auth
commands.description:
Manage authentication and login sessions

Examples:
  sorcha auth login --username admin@example.com --password <pass>
  sorcha auth status
  sorcha auth logout

commands.name: login
commands.description: Authenticate as a user or service principal

options.name: --password
options.description: Password for user authentication (INSECURE - use interactive mode instead)

options.name: --client-id
options.description: Client ID for service principal authentication

options.name: --client-secret
options.description: Client secret for service principal authentication (INSECURE - use interactive mode instead)

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Use interactive login (prompts for credentials securely) [default: True]

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to authenticate with (defaults to active profile)

options.name: --all
options.description: Clear tokens for all profiles (default: current profile only) [default: False]

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to logout from (defaults to active profile)

commands.name: status
commands.description: Check authentication status for the current profile

options.name: --profile
options.description: Profile to check status for (defaults to active profile)

commands.name: register
commands.description:
Manage registers (distributed ledgers)

Examples:
  sorcha register list
  sorcha register get --id <register-id>
  sorcha register create --name "My Register" --blueprint-id <id>
  sorcha register stats --id <register-id>

options.name: --owner-wallet
options.description: Owner wallet address for signing attestation

options.name: --advertise
options.description: Whether to advertise on peer network (true/false)

commands.name: blueprints
commands.description: List blueprints on the system register

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export register metadata and policy as JSON

commands.name: export-transactions
commands.description: Export register transactions as CSV or JSON

commands.name: tx
commands.description:
Manage transactions in registers

Examples:
  sorcha tx list --register-id <id>
  sorcha tx get --register-id <id> --id <tx-id>
  sorcha tx submit --register-id <id> --wallet <addr> --data '{"key":"value"}'

commands.name: submit
commands.description: Submit a new transaction to a register

commands.name: status
commands.description: Get the status of a transaction

commands.name: wallet
commands.description:
Manage cryptographic wallets

Examples:
  sorcha wallet list
  sorcha wallet create --name "My Wallet" --algorithm ED25519
  sorcha wallet sign --address <addr> --data <hex>

options.name: --word-count
options.description: Number of words in mnemonic (12, 15, 18, 21, or 24) [default: 12]

commands.name: recover
commands.description: Recover a wallet from mnemonic phrase

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Optional passphrase if one was used during creation

commands.name: sign
commands.description: Sign data with a wallet's private key

options.name: --subject
options.description: Subject (user ID) to grant access to

commands.name: list
commands.description: List access grants for a wallet

commands.name: check
commands.description: Check if a subject has access to a wallet

commands.name: create-batch
commands.description: Create multiple wallets in a batch

commands.name: docket
commands.description:
Inspect dockets (sealed blocks) in registers

Examples:
  sorcha docket list --register-id <id>
  sorcha docket get --register-id <id> --id <docket-id>
  sorcha docket transactions --register-id <id> --docket-number 1

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all dockets in a register

commands.name: query
commands.description:
Query transactions across registers

Examples:
  sorcha query wallet --address <wallet-addr>
  sorcha query sender --address <wallet-addr>
  sorcha query odata --query "$filter=Status eq 'Confirmed'"

commands.name: wallet
commands.description: Query transactions by wallet address (sender or recipient)

commands.name: sender
commands.description: Query transactions by sender wallet address

commands.name: peer
commands.description:
Monitor peer network and node health

Examples:
  sorcha peer list
  sorcha peer health
  sorcha peer subscriptions
  sorcha peer subscribe --register-id <id>

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all known peers in the network

commands.name: get
commands.description: Get detailed information about a specific peer

commands.name: quality
commands.description: Display connection quality scores for all peers

commands.name: subscriptions
commands.description: List register subscriptions and sync progress

commands.name: subscribe
commands.description: Subscribe to a register for replication

commands.name: blueprint
commands.description:
Manage blueprints (workflow definitions)

Examples:
  sorcha blueprint list
  sorcha blueprint create --file blueprint.json
  sorcha blueprint publish --id <id> --register-id <reg-id>

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a blueprint from a JSON file

commands.name: publish
commands.description: Publish a blueprint to a register

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export a blueprint definition as JSON

commands.name: participant
commands.description:
Manage participant identities

Examples:
  sorcha participant list --org-id <id>
  sorcha participant register --org-id <id> --display-name "Alice"
  sorcha participant search --query alice

commands.name: register
commands.description: Register a new participant in an organization

commands.name: wallet-link
commands.description: Initiate a wallet link challenge for a participant

commands.name: publish
commands.description: Publish a participant to a register

commands.name: credential
commands.description:
Manage verifiable credentials

Examples:
  sorcha credential list
  sorcha credential issue --type <type> --subject <did>
  sorcha credential verify --id <credential-id>

options.name: --expires-in-days
options.description: Number of days until credential expires

commands.name: present
commands.description: Present a credential to a verifier

options.name: --claims
options.description: Comma-separated list of claims to present (selective disclosure)

commands.name: status
commands.description: Get the status of a credential

options.name: --expires-in-days
options.description: Number of days until the refreshed credential expires

commands.name: get
commands.description: Get a W3C Bitstring Status List

commands.name: validator
commands.description:
Manage the validator service

Examples:
  sorcha validator status
  sorcha validator start
  sorcha validator metrics

commands.name: status
commands.description:
Get validator service status

Note: --register-id is required (breaking change from v1.0.x)

commands.name: process
commands.description: Process pending transactions for a register

commands.name: refresh
commands.description: Refresh the validator list for a register

commands.name: setup
commands.description: Set up threshold signing for a register

commands.name: admin
commands.description:
Administrative operations

Examples:
  sorcha admin health
  sorcha admin alerts
  sorcha admin events list

options.name: --severity
options.description: Filter by severity (Info, Warning, Error, Critical)

commands.name: schema
commands.description:
Schema management operations

Examples:
  sorcha schema providers list
  sorcha schema providers refresh --id <provider-id>

options.name: --name
options.description: Name of the schema provider to refresh

commands.name: operation
commands.description:
Encryption operation management

Examples:
  sorcha operation status --id <operation-id>

commands.name: action
commands.description:
Blueprint action execution

Examples:
  sorcha action execute --blueprint-id <id> --action-id <action> --data '{"key":"value"}'

options.name: --no-wait
options.description: Don't wait for async operations to complete [default: False]

commands.name: events
commands.description:
Real-time event streaming

Examples:
  sorcha events watch --all
  sorcha events watch --register <id>
  sorcha events watch --blueprints
  sorcha events watch --all --output json | jq .

commands.name: watch
commands.description: Stream real-time events from the platform

options.name: --register
options.description: Filter events for a specific register ID

options.name: --blueprints
options.description: Watch blueprint events only (BlueprintPublished, ActionCompleted) [default: False]

options.name: --all
options.description: Watch all events (register and blueprint) [default: False]

options.name: --since
options.description: Only show events after this timestamp (ISO 8601). Note: filtering is applied client-side

commands.name: invitation
commands.description:
Manage register invitations

Examples:
  sorcha invitation create --register-id <id> --target-org-did <did>
  sorcha invitation list --direction sent
  sorcha invitation accept --token <token>
  sorcha invitation revoke --id <id>

options.name: --expires-in
options.description: Expiration time in hours (default: server-side default)

commands.name: health
commands.description:
Check service health status

Examples:
  sorcha health
  sorcha health --service register
  sorcha health --service tenant

options.name: --service
options.description: Specific service to check (blueprint, register, tenant, wallet, validator, peer, gateway)

commands.name: verify
commands.description:
Verify transaction receipts and bundles

Examples:
  sorcha verify receipt --register-id <id> --tx-id <id>
  sorcha verify bundle --register-id <id> --tx-id <id>

commands.name: receipt
commands.description: Get and verify a transaction receipt

commands.name: bundle
commands.description: Get and verify a transaction verification bundle

commands.name: platform
commands.description:
Platform management (system admin)

Examples:
  sorcha platform orgs
  sorcha platform orgs --status active
  sorcha platform settings

commands.name: audit
commands.description:
Query and export audit logs

Examples:
  sorcha audit list --org-id <id>
  sorcha audit list --org-id <id> --since 2026-01-01 --action Login
  sorcha audit export --org-id <id> --output audit.json

commands.name: list
commands.description:
List audit log entries

Examples:
  sorcha audit list --org-id <id>
  sorcha audit list --org-id <id> --since 2026-01-01 --until 2026-03-31
  sorcha audit list --org-id <id> --action Login --user admin@example.com

options.name: --since
options.description: Filter entries from this date (ISO 8601, e.g., 2026-01-01)

options.name: --until
options.description: Filter entries until this date (ISO 8601, e.g., 2026-03-31)

options.name: --action
options.description: Filter by action type (e.g., Login, CreateOrg, UpdateUser)

commands.name: export
commands.description:
Export audit log entries to a file

Examples:
  sorcha audit export --org-id <id> --output audit.json
  sorcha audit export --org-id <id> --output audit.csv --format csv
  sorcha audit export --org-id <id> --output audit.json --since 2026-01-01

commands.name: config
commands.description:
Manage CLI configuration and profiles

Examples:
  sorcha config list
  sorcha config init --profile prod --url https://sorcha.example.com
  sorcha config view
  sorcha config validate
  sorcha config export --output config.json

options.name: --service-url
options.description: Base URL for all services (recommended - e.g., 'http://localhost')

options.name: --check-connectivity
options.description: Verify connectivity to service URLs [default: True]

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the profile to activate

commands.name: view
commands.description:
Display current configuration details

Examples:
  sorcha config view
  sorcha config view --output json

commands.name: validate
commands.description:
Check connectivity to all configured services

Examples:
  sorcha config validate
  sorcha config validate --output json

commands.name: export
commands.description:
Export current configuration to a file

Examples:
  sorcha config export --output config.json
  sorcha config export --output config.yaml --format yaml

commands.name: completion
commands.description:
Generate shell completion scripts

Examples:
  sorcha completion bash >> ~/.bashrc
  sorcha completion zsh >> ~/.zshrc
  sorcha completion powershell >> $PROFILE
  sorcha completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/sorcha.fish

arguments.name: shell
arguments.description: Shell type: bash, zsh, powershell, or fish

commands.name: help
commands.description: Getting started guide for the Sorcha CLI
